---
title: "VotingIssuesPoll2020Analysis"
author: "Isabelle Jensen"
date: "2026-03-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(data.table)
library(knitr)
library(lubridate)
library(janitor)
library(gmodels)
library(gt)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)


library(haven)
issue <- read_sav("2020-08-31 Post-UMD Voting Issues Data.sav")

glimpse(issue)
table(issue$PPWORKA)

library(sjlabelled)

get_label(issue)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r labeling}




issue <- issue %>%
  rename(
    vote_pref_method = Q3,
    vote_pref_timing = Q4
  )


issue <- issue %>%
  rename(
    conf_own_vote = Q18,
    conf_inperson_elecday = Q19_1,
    conf_inperson_early = Q19_2,
    conf_mail = Q19_3,
    conf_state = Q20,
    conf_national = Q21
  )



issue <- issue %>%
  rename(
    usps_job_rating = Q22,
    usps_ballot_confidence = Q23,
    usps_mail_frequency = Q24,
    usps_less_mail = Q25_1,
    usps_slower = Q25_2,
    usps_late_delivery = Q25_3
  )


issue <- issue %>%
  rename(
    vote_fair_white_black = Q40,
    vote_fair_white_hispanic = Q41
  )
library(dplyr)
library(sjlabelled)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    PARTYID_num = as.numeric(PARTYID),
    IDEO_num = as.numeric(IDEO)
  )

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    dem = case_when(
      PARTYID_num == 1 ~ 1,   # Democrat
      PARTYID_num == 2 ~ 0,   # Republican
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
table(issue$dem, useNA = "ifany")

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    IDEO_clean = ifelse(IDEO_num == -1, NA, IDEO_num)
  )
summary(issue$IDEO_clean)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    PARTYID_clean = ifelse(PARTYID_num == -1, NA, PARTYID_num)
  )

table(issue$PARTYID_clean, useNA="ifany")
table(issue$dem, useNA="ifany")
summary(issue$IDEO_clean)

```



```{r}


#preference for how to cast vote in this fall's presidential election
#How confident are you that the U.S. Postal Service will deliver mailed election ballots in a timely manner for this fall’s presidential election?
#create vote by mail or not binary
library(dplyr)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    vote_pref_method_clean = na_if(vote_pref_method, -1),
    mail_pref = ifelse(vote_pref_method_clean == 2, 1,
                       ifelse(vote_pref_method_clean %in% c(1, 3), 0, NA))
  )


model1 <- glm(mail_pref ~ usps_ballot_confidence + PARTYID + IDEO,
              family = binomial,
              data = issue)

summary(model1)
#People with less confidence in the timely deliver of their ballot are significantly less likely to use a mail in ballot coeff -0.476 and significant


####looking at general performance rating of the usps and plan to vote by mail in fall####

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    usps_job_clean = na_if(usps_job_rating, -1)
  )

table(issue$usps_job_clean, useNA = "ifany")


model2 <- glm(mail_pref ~ 
                usps_job_clean + 
                PARTYID + IDEO,
              family = binomial,
              data = issue)

summary(model2)


model2_weighted <- glm(
  mail_pref ~ usps_job_clean + dem + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = issue,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model2_weighted)
glimpse(issue$usps_job_clean)

#negative significant relationship between job evaluation and deciding to vote by mail
#as usps evaluations get worse probability of preferring mail decreases
#-0.266
#usps job is reverse coded

##Do people express higher USPS ballot confidence in states they believe have more flexible vote-by-mail policies?####
glimpse(issue)

library(dplyr)
library(MASS)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    # USPS ballot confidence DV (Q23) - set -1 to NA
    usps_ballot_confidence_clean = na_if(usps_ballot_confidence, -1),

    # Q14 policy type: remove -1 and "Not sure" (=4)
    mail_policy_type_clean = na_if(na_if(Q14, -1), 4),

    # Q15 deadline: remove -1 and "Not sure" (very likely = 5 in your file)
    mail_deadline_rule_clean = na_if(na_if(Q15, -1), 5),

    # Q16 eligibility: remove -1 and "Not sure" (=3)
    mail_eligibility_rule_clean = na_if(na_if(Q16, -1), 3)
  )

table(issue$Q15, useNA="ifany")

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    mail_deadline_clean = na_if(na_if(Q15, -1), 5)
  )

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    mail_deadline_flex = case_when(
      mail_deadline_clean == 1 ~ 4,
      mail_deadline_clean == 2 ~ 3,
      mail_deadline_clean == 3 ~ 2,
      mail_deadline_clean == 4 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    mail_eligibility_binary = case_when(
      Q16 == 1 ~ 1,
      Q16 == 2 ~ 0,
      Q16 %in% c(3, -1) ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
glimpse(issue$Q16)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    usps_ballot_conf_clean = na_if(usps_ballot_confidence, -1)
  )

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    usps_ballot_conf_rev = 5 - usps_ballot_conf_clean
  )



model_deadline <- lm(usps_ballot_conf_rev ~ 
                       mail_deadline_flex + 
                       PARTYID + IDEO,
                     data = issue)

summary(model_deadline)
#confidence in usps is not effected significantly by deadline




######interesting#####
model_eligibility <- lm(usps_ballot_conf_rev ~ 
                          mail_eligibility_binary + 
                          PARTYID + IDEO,
                        data = issue)

summary(model_eligibility)
#"State allows any eligible citizen to vote by mail/absentee" "State only allows mail/absentee voting under certain conditions"
#the eligibility flexibility makes usps confidence higher
#the easier it is to be eligible for a mail in ballot the more people trust the usps to get the ballots there on time
#postiive coeff 0.16683 significant p value 0.00617


model_eligibility2 <- lm(mail_eligibility_binary ~ 
                          usps_ballot_conf_rev + 
                          PARTYID + IDEO,
                        data = issue)

summary(model_eligibility2)
#causality would be hard to make sense of here

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    mail_program_clean = case_when(
      Q14 %in% c(1,2,3) ~ Q14,
      Q14 %in% c(4, -1) ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

table(issue$mail_program_clean, useNA = "ifany")
glimpse(issue$mail_program_clean)






issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    mail_program_clean = case_when(
      Q14 %in% c(1,2,3) ~ Q14,
      Q14 %in% c(4,-1) ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

table(issue$mail_program_clean, useNA = "ifany")


issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    mail_program_binary = case_when(
      mail_program_clean == 1 ~ 0,
      mail_program_clean %in% c(2,3) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

table(issue$mail_program_binary, useNA = "ifany")

model_program_binary_w <- lm(
  usps_ballot_conf_rev ~ 
    mail_program_binary +
    dem + 
    IDEO_clean,
  data = issue,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model_program_binary_w)









model_program_extent <- lm(usps_ballot_conf_rev ~ 
                             mail_program_clean +
                             PARTYID + IDEO,
                           data = issue)

summary(model_program_extent)
#Skipped" "Voters must contact their elections office to request a mail-in ballot" "All voters in your state will be automatically sent a ballot-request form" "All voters in your state will automatically be sent an election ballot" ...
#the more expansive the mail program is the more people trust the usps
#significant and positive coeff 0.0755 and p value 0.00697

model_program_extent_w <- lm(usps_ballot_conf_rev ~ 
                               mail_program_clean +
                               PARTYID + IDEO,
                             data = issue,
                             weights = WEIGHT)

summary(model_program_extent_w)

#less significant with weights


table(issue$ppethm, useNA = "ifany")


table(issue$vote_fair_white_black, useNA = "ifany")


summary(glm(mail_pref ~ vbmever + usps_ballot_confidence + PARTYID,
    family = binomial, data = issue))
#vote by mail ever and usps ballot confidence have a negative relationship with mail pref
table(issue$mail_pref)






```

Call:
lm(formula = usps_ballot_conf_rev ~ mail_eligibility_binary + 
    PARTYID + IDEO, data = issue)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-1.97992 -0.76571  0.09653  0.27521  1.35056 

Coefficients:
                        Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)              2.73775    0.09038  30.290  < 2e-16 ***
mail_eligibility_binary  0.16683    0.06082   2.743  0.00617 ** 
PARTYID                  0.02907    0.02694   1.079  0.28083    
IDEO                    -0.01185    0.01490  -0.795  0.42684    
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1



Call:
lm(formula = usps_ballot_conf_rev ~ mail_program_clean + PARTYID + 
    IDEO, data = issue, weights = WEIGHT)

Weighted Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-2.9827 -0.5468  0.1153  0.3143  2.5393 

Coefficients:
                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)         2.849304   0.096055  29.663   <2e-16 ***
mail_program_clean  0.057152   0.029327   1.949   0.0515 .  
PARTYID             0.004911   0.026404   0.186   0.8525    
IDEO               -0.029698   0.015235  -1.949   0.0515 .  

Call:
glm(formula = mail_pref ~ usps_job_clean + PARTYID + IDEO, family = binomial, 
    data = issue, weights = WEIGHT)

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)     0.52422    0.19543   2.682  0.00731 ** 
usps_job_clean -0.18820    0.06202  -3.035  0.00241 ** 
PARTYID         0.05130    0.05262   0.975  0.32958    
IDEO           -0.22633    0.03263  -6.936 4.04e-12 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)


```{r usps graphs}



library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)

pred <- ggpredict(model2_weighted, terms = "usps_job_clean")

ggplot(pred, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = .2, fill = "steelblue") +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Excellent",
      "Good",
      "Only fair",
      "Poor"
    )
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Evaluation of USPS Job Performance",
    y = "Predicted Probability of Preferring Mail Voting",
    title = "Preference for Mail Voting by USPS Job Evaluation"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )






library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

policy_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(!is.na(mail_eligibility_binary),
         !is.na(usps_ballot_conf_rev),
         !is.na(WEIGHT)) %>%
  group_by(mail_eligibility_binary) %>%
  summarise(
    confidence = weighted.mean(usps_ballot_conf_rev, WEIGHT, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

ggplot(policy_plot, aes(x = factor(mail_eligibility_binary), y = confidence)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange", width = .6) +

  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "State restricts mail voting",
      "Any voter can vote by mail"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1,4),
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Very confident",
      "Somewhat confident",
      "Not too confident",
      "Not confident"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "USPS Ballot Delivery Confidence by Mail Voting Eligibility",
    x = "Perceived Mail Voting Eligibility Rule",
    y = "Confidence USPS Delivers Ballots"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


policy_plot
table(issue$mail_eligibility_binary, useNA = "ifany")
summary(issue$usps_ballot_conf_rev)
summary(issue$WEIGHT)




library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

policy_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(mail_eligibility_binary),
    !is.na(usps_ballot_conf_rev),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    eligibility_label = ifelse(
      mail_eligibility_binary == 1,
      "Any voter can vote by mail",
      "State restricts mail voting"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(eligibility_label) %>%
  summarise(
    confidence = weighted.mean(usps_ballot_conf_rev, WEIGHT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(policy_plot)

ggplot(policy_plot, aes(x = eligibility_label, y = confidence)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkorange", width = 0.6) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 4)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Not at all confident",
      "Not too confident",
      "Somewhat confident",
      "Very confident"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "USPS Ballot Delivery Confidence by Mail Voting Eligibility",
    x = "Perceived Mail Voting Eligibility Rule",
    y = "Confidence USPS Delivers Ballots"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1)
  )





library(ggeffects)

pred_interaction <- ggpredict(
  model2_weighted,
  terms = c("usps_job_clean", "IDEO")
)

ggplot(pred_interaction,
       aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group)) +

  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Excellent",
      "Good",
      "Only fair",
      "Poor"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format()
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Mail Voting Preference by USPS Trust and Ideology",
    x = "Evaluation of USPS Job Performance",
    y = "Predicted Probability of Preferring Mail Voting",
    color = "Ideology"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )







library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggeffects)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    IDEO_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(IDEO) < 0, NA, as.numeric(IDEO)),
    usps_job_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(usps_job_clean) < 0, NA, as.numeric(usps_job_clean))
  )

model2_weighted_clean <- glm(
  mail_pref ~ usps_job_clean + IDEO_clean + PARTYID,
  family = binomial,
  data = issue,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

pred_interaction <- ggpredict(
  model2_weighted_clean,
  terms = c("usps_job_clean", "IDEO_clean [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]")
)

pred_interaction$group <- factor(
  pred_interaction$group,
  levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7"),
  labels = c(
    "Extremely Liberal",
    "Liberal",
    "Slightly Liberal",
    "Moderate",
    "Slightly Conservative",
    "Conservative",
    "Extremely Conservative"
  )
)

ggplot(pred_interaction, aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group, fill = group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.12, color = NA) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c("Excellent", "Good", "Only fair", "Poor")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Preferring Mail Voting by USPS Evaluation and Ideology",
    x = "Evaluation of USPS Job Performance",
    y = "Predicted Probability of Preferring Mail Voting",
    color = "Ideology",
    fill = "Ideology"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right"
  )





library(dplyr)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    PARTY_clean = case_when(
      PARTYID == 1 ~ "Democrat",
      PARTYID == 2 ~ "Republican",
      PARTYID == 3 ~ "Independent",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

model_party <- glm(
  mail_pref ~ usps_job_clean + PARTY_clean + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = issue,
  weights = WEIGHT
)


library(ggeffects)

pred_party <- ggpredict(
  model_party,
  terms = c("usps_job_clean", "PARTY_clean")
)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(pred_party,
       aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group, fill = group)) +

  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +

  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    alpha = 0.15,
    color = NA
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Excellent",
      "Good",
      "Only fair",
      "Poor"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Democrat" = "blue",
      "Republican" = "red",
      "Independent" = "purple"
    )
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Democrat" = "blue",
      "Republican" = "red",
      "Independent" = "purple"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Preferring Mail Voting 
    by USPS Evaluation and Party",
    x = "Evaluation of USPS Job Performance",
    y = "Predicted Probability of Preferring Mail Voting",
    color = "Party",
    fill = "Party"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right"
  )



library(modelsummary)

modelplot(model2_weighted) +
  labs(
    title = "Predictors of Mail Voting Preference",
    x = "Coefficient Estimate (Log Odds)",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

```


```{r racial}
####racial#####

library(dplyr)

issue_clean <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    race_clean = na_if(ppethm, -1),
    vote_fair_white_black_clean = na_if(vote_fair_white_black, -1)
  )

table(issue_clean$race_clean,
      issue_clean$vote_fair_white_black_clean)



prop.table(
  table(issue_clean$race_clean,
        issue_clean$vote_fair_white_black_clean),
  margin = 1
)


issue_clean <- issue_clean %>%
  mutate(
    race_factor = factor(race_clean,
                         levels = c(1,2,3,4,5),
                         labels = c("White",
                                    "Black",
                                    "Other",
                                    "Hispanic",
                                    "Multiracial")),
    
    fairness_factor = factor(vote_fair_white_black_clean,
                             levels = c(1,2,3),
                             labels = c("Easier for White",
                                        "Easier for Black",
                                        "Equal treatment"))
  )


library(janitor)

issue_clean %>%
  tabyl(race_factor, fairness_factor) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)


chisq.test(table(issue_clean$race_factor,
                 issue_clean$fairness_factor))


issue_clean <- issue_clean %>%
  mutate(
    vote_fair_white_hispanic_clean = na_if(vote_fair_white_hispanic, -1),
    fairness_hispanic_factor = factor(vote_fair_white_hispanic_clean,
                                       levels = c(1,2,3),
                                       labels = c("Easier for White",
                                                  "Easier for Hispanic",
                                                  "Equal treatment"))
  )

issue_clean %>%
  tabyl(race_factor, fairness_hispanic_factor) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)



#Black respondents are much more likely than White respondents to believe elections make it easier for White citizens to vote.

library(dplyr)

issue_model <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    fairness_clean = case_when(
      vote_fair_white_black %in% c(1,2,3) ~ vote_fair_white_black,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(fairness_clean))
issue_model$fairness_factor <- factor(issue_model$fairness_clean,
                                       levels = c(3,1,2),
                                       labels = c("Equal treatment",
                                                  "Easier for White",
                                                  "Easier for Black"))

issue_model$fairness_factor <- factor(issue_model$fairness_clean,
                                       levels = c(3,1,2),
                                       labels = c("Equal treatment",
                                                  "Easier for White",
                                                  "Easier for Black"))




library(dplyr)

issue_model <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    fairness_black_binary = case_when(
      vote_fair_white_black == 1 ~ 1,   # Easier for White
      vote_fair_white_black == 3 ~ 0,   # Equal treatment
      vote_fair_white_black %in% c(2, -1) ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
issue_model <- issue_model %>%
  mutate(
    fairness_hisp_binary = case_when(
      vote_fair_white_hispanic == 1 ~ 1,
      vote_fair_white_hispanic == 3 ~ 0,
      vote_fair_white_hispanic %in% c(2, -1) ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

table(issue_model$fairness_black_binary, useNA="ifany")
table(issue_model$fairness_hisp_binary, useNA="ifany")
issue_model <- issue_model %>%
  mutate(
    race_factor = factor(ppethm,
                         levels = c(1,2,3,4,5),
                         labels = c("White",
                                    "Black",
                                    "Other",
                                    "Hispanic",
                                    "Multiracial"))
  )

model_black_race <- glm(fairness_black_binary ~ race_factor,
                        family = binomial,
                        data = issue_model)

summary(model_black_race)
#every race (using white as the comparison) was more likely to say that white people are advantaged at the polls except hispanics which were not meaningfully different from whites in this



model_black_controls <- glm(fairness_black_binary ~ 
                              race_factor +
                              PARTYID +
                              IDEO,
                            family = binomial,
                            data = issue_model)

summary(model_black_controls)
#trend continues with controls


model_hisp_controls <- glm(fairness_hisp_binary ~ 
                             race_factor +
                             PARTYID +
                             IDEO,
                           family = binomial,
                           data = issue_model)

summary(model_hisp_controls)

#even when asked about hispanics hispanics dont seem to think they are less advantaged than whites in the polls but every other race does 


exp(coef(model_black_controls))




table(issue$ppethm)



issue_model <- issue_model %>%
  mutate(
    white = ifelse(ppethm == 1, 1, 0),
    black = ifelse(ppethm == 2, 1, 0),
    hispanic = ifelse(ppethm == 3, 1, 0)
  )


issue_model <- issue_model %>%
  mutate(
    white = ifelse(is.na(ppethm), NA, ifelse(ppethm == 1, 1, 0)),
    black = ifelse(is.na(ppethm), NA, ifelse(ppethm == 2, 1, 0)),
    hispanic = ifelse(is.na(ppethm), NA, ifelse(ppethm == 3, 1, 0))
  )

model_black <- glm(fairness_black_binary ~ white + black + hispanic +
                   PARTYID + IDEO,
                   family = binomial,
                   data = issue_model)

summary(model_black)



model_hisp <- glm(fairness_hisp_binary ~ white + black + hispanic +
                  PARTYID + IDEO,
                  family = binomial,
                  data = issue_model)
summary(model_hisp)



model_black_weighted2 <- glm(
  fairness_black_binary ~ white + black + hispanic + PARTYID + IDEO,
  family = binomial,
  data = issue_model,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

model_hisp_weighted2 <- glm(
  fairness_hisp_binary ~ white + black + hispanic + PARTYID + IDEO,
  family = binomial,
  data = issue_model,
  weights = WEIGHT
)
summary(model_black_weighted2)
summary(model_hisp_weighted2)





library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

issue_plot <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black",
      ppethm == 3 ~ "Other",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic",
      ppethm == 5 ~ "Multiracial",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    fairness_black = case_when(
      as.numeric(vote_fair_white_black) == 1 ~ "Easier for White",
      as.numeric(vote_fair_white_black) == 2 ~ "Easier for Black",
      as.numeric(vote_fair_white_black) == 3 ~ "Equal treatment",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    fairness_hisp = case_when(
      as.numeric(vote_fair_white_hispanic) == 1 ~ "Easier for White",
      as.numeric(vote_fair_white_hispanic) == 2 ~ "Easier for Hispanic",
      as.numeric(vote_fair_white_hispanic) == 3 ~ "Equal treatment",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )



black_plot_data <- issue_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(race_group), !is.na(fairness_black)) %>%
  count(race_group, fairness_black) %>%
  group_by(race_group) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100)

ggplot(black_plot_data, aes(x = race_group, y = percent, fill = fairness_black)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Perceptions of Whether Voting Is Easier for White or Black Citizens",
    x = "Respondent Race",
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Response"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)





hisp_plot_data <- issue_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(race_group), !is.na(fairness_hisp)) %>%
  count(race_group, fairness_hisp) %>%
  group_by(race_group) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100)



ggplot(hisp_plot_data, aes(x = race_group, y = percent, fill = fairness_hisp)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Perceptions of Whether Voting Is Easier for White or Hispanic Citizens",
    x = "Respondent Race",
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Response"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

```

race_factor Easier for White Easier for Black Equal treatment  NA_
       White            35.2%             0.9%           62.7% 1.2%
       Black            75.0%             0.0%           22.1% 2.9%
       Other            44.4%             3.7%           49.4% 2.5%
    Hispanic            38.5%             2.2%           57.0% 2.2%
 Multiracial            55.8%             3.8%           40.4% 0.0%
  race_factor Easier for White Easier for Hispanic Equal treatment  NA_
       White            36.7%                1.1%           61.0% 1.2%
       Black            71.5%                2.3%           23.8% 2.3%
       Other            48.1%                6.2%           44.4% 1.2%
    Hispanic            43.7%                2.2%           51.9% 2.2%
 Multiracial            55.8%                1.9%           42.3% 0.0%



Call:
glm(formula = fairness_black_binary ~ race_factor, family = binomial, 
    data = issue_model)

Coefficients:
                       Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)            -0.57691    0.05459 -10.568  < 2e-16 ***
race_factorBlack        1.79914    0.19248   9.347  < 2e-16 ***
race_factorOther        0.47155    0.23613   1.997  0.04583 *  
race_factorHispanic     0.18435    0.18761   0.983  0.32579    
race_factorMultiracial  0.89969    0.29169   3.084  0.00204 ** 
## Including Plots

Call:
glm(formula = fairness_hisp_binary ~ race_factor + PARTYID + 
    IDEO, family = binomial, data = issue_model)

Coefficients:
                       Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)             3.05831    0.21413  14.282  < 2e-16 ***
race_factorBlack        1.43317    0.20755   6.905 5.02e-12 ***
race_factorOther        0.67903    0.26152   2.596  0.00942 ** 
race_factorHispanic     0.16936    0.21325   0.794  0.42708    
race_factorMultiracial  0.63322    0.32437   1.952  0.05092 .  
PARTYID                -0.11181    0.06079  -1.839  0.06586 .  
IDEO                   -0.81439    0.04555 -17.879  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1



Call:
glm(formula = fairness_black_binary ~ white + black + hispanic + 
    PARTYID + IDEO, family = binomial, data = issue_model)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)  3.31757    0.26130  12.696  < 2e-16 ***
white       -0.16726    0.18543  -0.902   0.3671    
black        1.51371    0.26601   5.690 1.27e-08 ***
hispanic     0.32083    0.30992   1.035   0.3006    
PARTYID     -0.12155    0.06150  -1.977   0.0481 *  
IDEO        -0.85545    0.04711 -18.160  < 2e-16 ***


Call:
glm(formula = fairness_hisp_binary ~ white + black + hispanic + 
    PARTYID + IDEO, family = binomial, data = issue_model)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)  3.35334    0.25910  12.942  < 2e-16 ***
white       -0.30446    0.18216  -1.671   0.0946 .  
black        1.13074    0.26018   4.346 1.39e-05 ***
hispanic     0.37362    0.30634   1.220   0.2226    
PARTYID     -0.10637    0.06058  -1.756   0.0791 .  
IDEO        -0.81496    0.04553 -17.900  < 2e-16 ***


Call:
glm(formula = fairness_black_binary ~ white + black + hispanic + 
    PARTYID + IDEO, family = binomial, data = issue_model, weights = WEIGHT)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)  2.79018    0.22908  12.180  < 2e-16 ***
white       -0.07415    0.15607  -0.475   0.6347    
black        1.42502    0.21485   6.633  3.3e-11 ***
hispanic     0.25960    0.26374   0.984   0.3250    
PARTYID     -0.10481    0.05863  -1.788   0.0738 .  
IDEO        -0.76652    0.04514 -16.979  < 2e-16 ***
---

Call:
glm(formula = fairness_hisp_binary ~ white + black + hispanic + 
    PARTYID + IDEO, family = binomial, data = issue_model, weights = WEIGHT)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)  2.87595    0.22787  12.621  < 2e-16 ***
white       -0.35843    0.15213  -2.356   0.0185 *  
black        0.99223    0.21067   4.710 2.48e-06 ***
hispanic     0.24456    0.25957   0.942   0.3461    
PARTYID     -0.07209    0.05757  -1.252   0.2105    
IDEO        -0.71518    0.04312 -16.588  < 2e-16 ***
```{r fraud and suppression}

glimpse(issue$PARTYID)
glimpse(issue$IDEO)
glimpse(issue$fraud_perception)
glimpse(issue$Q27)
glimpse(issue$Q28)
glimpse(issue$Q29)


library(dplyr)
library(survey)
library(sjlabelled)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

design_issue <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~WEIGHT,
  data = issue
)


issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    fraud_perception = ifelse(as.numeric(Q27) < 0, NA, as.numeric(Q27)),
    suppression_perception = ifelse(as.numeric(Q28) < 0, NA, as.numeric(Q28)),
    misinformation_perception = ifelse(as.numeric(Q28a) < 0, NA, as.numeric(Q28a))
  )
issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    fraud_perception_rev = 5 - fraud_perception,
    suppression_perception_rev = 5 - suppression_perception,
    misinformation_perception_rev = 5 - misinformation_perception
  )

design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  fraud_perception_rev = issue$fraud_perception_rev,
  suppression_perception_rev = issue$suppression_perception_rev,
  misinformation_perception_rev = issue$misinformation_perception_rev
)


model_fraud <- svyglm(
  fraud_perception_rev ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_fraud)
#significant for both
#Republicans believe there is more fraud and so do conservatives


model_suppression <- svyglm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_suppression)
#suppression is significant for both party and ideo
#democrats believe there is more supression 


issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    fraud_supp_gap = fraud_perception_rev - suppression_perception_rev
  )

design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  fraud_supp_gap = issue$fraud_supp_gap
)


model_gap <- svyglm(
  fraud_supp_gap ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_gap)






fraud_supp_plot <- svyby(
  ~fraud_perception_rev + suppression_perception_rev,
  ~IDEO_clean,
  design_issue,
  svymean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

ggplot(fraud_supp_plot) +
  geom_line(aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = fraud_perception_rev, color = "Voter Fraud"), linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = suppression_perception_rev, color = "Voter Suppression"), linewidth = 1.2) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 4),
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Rarely",
      "Occasionally",
      "Somewhat often",
      "Very often"
    )
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Mean Perceptions of Voter Fraud and Suppression by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Perceived Frequency",
    color = "Perception"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 35, size = 9),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right"
  )




ggplot(issue, aes(x = IDEO_clean, weight = WEIGHT)) +
  
  geom_smooth(aes(y = fraud_perception_rev, color = "Voter Fraud"),
              method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  
  geom_smooth(aes(y = suppression_perception_rev, color = "Voter Suppression"),
              method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1,4),
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c("Rarely", "Occasionally", "Somewhat often", "Very often")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Regression of Election Threat Perceptions by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Perceived Frequency",
    color = "Perception"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )




ggplot(issue, aes(x = IDEO_clean, weight = WEIGHT)) +
  
  geom_smooth(
    aes(y = fraud_perception_rev, color = "Voter Fraud", fill = "Voter Fraud"),
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE,
    alpha = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  
  geom_smooth(
    aes(y = suppression_perception_rev, color = "Voter Suppression", fill = "Voter Suppression"),
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE,
    alpha = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1,4),
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Rarely",
      "Occasionally",
      "Somewhat often",
      "Very often"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Regression Perceptions of Voter Fraud and Suppression by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Perceived Frequency",
    color = "Perception",
    fill = "Perception"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

```
Call:
svyglm(formula = fraud_supp_gap ~ dem + IDEO_clean, design = design_issue)

Survey design:
update(design_issue, fraud_supp_gap = issue$fraud_supp_gap)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept) -1.26268    0.18708  -6.749 2.33e-11 ***
dem         -0.82011    0.11097  -7.391 2.77e-13 ***
IDEO_clean   0.38070    0.03362  11.322  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Democrats are much more likely to believe voter suppression occurs more often than voter fraud.


More conservative respondents are more likely to believe voter fraud occurs more often than voter suppression.

Or equivalently:

More liberal respondents are more likely to believe voter suppression is the bigger problem.



Democrats and liberals tend to believe voter suppression is more common than voter fraud.

Republicans and conservatives are more likely to believe voter fraud is more common than voter suppression.


```{r confidence }
#    fraud_perception = ifelse(as.numeric(Q27) < 0, NA, as.numeric(Q27)),
issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    conf_own_vote_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(conf_own_vote) < 0, NA, as.numeric(conf_own_vote)),
    conf_inperson_elecday_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(conf_inperson_elecday) < 0, NA, as.numeric(conf_inperson_elecday)),
    conf_inperson_early_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(conf_inperson_early) < 0, NA, as.numeric(conf_inperson_early)),
    conf_mail_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(conf_mail) < 0, NA, as.numeric(conf_mail)),
    conf_state_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(conf_state) < 0, NA, as.numeric(conf_state)),
    conf_national_clean = ifelse(as.numeric(conf_national) < 0, NA, as.numeric(conf_national))
  )

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    conf_own_vote_rev = 5 - conf_own_vote_clean,
    conf_inperson_elecday_rev = 5 - conf_inperson_elecday_clean,
    conf_inperson_early_rev = 5 - conf_inperson_early_clean,
    conf_mail_rev = 5 - conf_mail_clean,
    conf_state_rev = 5 - conf_state_clean,
    conf_national_rev = 5 - conf_national_clean
  )
issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    mail_vs_inperson = conf_mail_rev - conf_inperson_elecday_rev
  )
design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  conf_own_vote_rev = issue$conf_own_vote_rev,
  conf_inperson_elecday_rev = issue$conf_inperson_elecday_rev,
  conf_inperson_early_rev = issue$conf_inperson_early_rev,
  conf_mail_rev = issue$conf_mail_rev,
  conf_state_rev = issue$conf_state_rev,
  conf_national_rev = issue$conf_national_rev,
  mail_vs_inperson_gap = issue$mail_vs_inperson_gap
)




design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  mail_vs_inperson = issue$mail_vs_inperson
)


model_confidence <- svyglm(
  mail_vs_inperson ~ dem + IDEO_clean + vbmever,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_confidence)

svyhist(
  ~mail_vs_inperson,
  design_issue,
  main = "Trust in Mail vs In-Person Voting",
  xlab = "Mail Confidence - In-Person Confidence"
)




design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  conf_mail_rev = issue$conf_mail_rev,
  conf_inperson_elecday_rev = issue$conf_inperson_elecday_rev,
  conf_inperson_early_rev = issue$conf_inperson_early_rev,
  PARTYID = issue$PARTYID,
  IDEO_clean = issue$IDEO_clean
)


model_conf_electionday <- svyglm(
  conf_inperson_elecday_rev ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_conf_electionday)

##not significant




model_conf_mail <- svyglm(
  conf_mail_rev ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_conf_mail)
#dems and liberals trust mail more significantly


model_conf_early <- svyglm(
  conf_inperson_early_rev ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_conf_early)

#not significant






library(survey)
library(dplyr)

conf_plot <- svyby(
  ~conf_inperson_elecday_rev +
   conf_inperson_early_rev +
   conf_mail_rev,
  ~IDEO_clean,
  design_issue,
  svymean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)


library(tidyr)

conf_plot_long <- conf_plot %>%
  dplyr::select(
    IDEO_clean,
    conf_inperson_elecday_rev,
    conf_inperson_early_rev,
    conf_mail_rev
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -IDEO_clean,
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "confidence"
  )


conf_plot_long$method <- recode(
  conf_plot_long$method,
  conf_inperson_elecday_rev = "Election Day Voting",
  conf_inperson_early_rev = "Early In-Person Voting",
  conf_mail_rev = "Mail Voting"
)


library(ggplot2)

ggplot(conf_plot_long,
       aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = confidence, color = method)) +

  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1,4),
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Not at all confident",
      "Not too confident",
      "Somewhat confident",
      "Very confident"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Mean Confidence in Voting Methods by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Confidence",
    color = "Voting Method"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 35, size = 9)
  )



```
Call:
svyglm(formula = conf_mail_rev ~ dem + IDEO_clean, design = design_issue)

Survey design:
update(design_issue, conf_mail_rev = issue$conf_mail_rev, conf_inperson_elecday_rev = issue$conf_inperson_elecday_rev, 
    conf_inperson_early_rev = issue$conf_inperson_early_rev, 
    PARTYID = issue$PARTYID, IDEO_clean = issue$IDEO_clean)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  3.22724    0.16095  20.052  < 2e-16 ***
dem          0.23953    0.09772   2.451   0.0144 *  
IDEO_clean  -0.12700    0.02886  -4.400 1.18e-05 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1



Democrats score about 0.24 points higher than Republicans on the mail-vs-in-person confidence gap, controlling for ideology and prior vote-by-mail experience.

Substantively:

Democrats trust mail voting more relative to in-person Election Day voting than Republicans do.


More conservative respondents trust mail voting less relative to in-person voting.

Or equivalently:

More liberal respondents are relatively more favorable toward mail voting confidence.


People without prior vote-by-mail experience have a mail-vs-in-person confidence score that is about 0.39 points lower.

Substantively:

People who have voted by mail before are more confident in mail voting relative to in-person voting.

```{r trust in politicians}

library(dplyr)
library(survey)
library(ggplot2)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    trust_legislature = ifelse(as.numeric(Q39_1) < 0, NA, 5 - as.numeric(Q39_1)),
    trust_state_elections = ifelse(as.numeric(Q39_3) < 0, NA, 5 - as.numeric(Q39_3)),
    trust_local_elections = ifelse(as.numeric(Q39_4) < 0, NA, 5 - as.numeric(Q39_4)),
    trust_president = ifelse(as.numeric(Q39_5) < 0, NA, 5 - as.numeric(Q39_5)),
    trust_congress = ifelse(as.numeric(Q39_6) < 0, NA, 5 - as.numeric(Q39_6))
  )

design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  trust_legislature = issue$trust_legislature,
  trust_president = issue$trust_president,
  trust_congress = issue$trust_congress,
  trust_state_elections = issue$trust_state_elections,
  trust_local_elections = issue$trust_local_elections
)

model_trust_elections <- svyglm(
  trust_state_elections ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_trust_elections)

svymean(
  ~trust_legislature +
    trust_president +
    trust_congress +
    trust_state_elections +
    trust_local_elections,
  design_issue,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

svyby(
  ~conf_mail_rev + conf_inperson_elecday_rev,
  ~IDEO_clean,
  design_issue,
  svymean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

conf_plot <- svyby(
  ~conf_mail_rev + conf_inperson_elecday_rev,
  ~IDEO_clean,
  design_issue,
  svymean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

ggplot(conf_plot) +
  geom_line(aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = conf_mail_rev, color = "Mail Voting")) +
  geom_line(aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = conf_inperson_elecday_rev, color = "Election Day")) +
  labs(
    title = "Confidence in Voting Methods by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Weighted Confidence"
  )


ggplot(conf_plot) +
  geom_line(aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = conf_mail_rev, color = "Mail Voting"), linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = conf_inperson_elecday_rev, color = "Election Day"), linewidth = 1.2) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1,4),
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Not at all confident",
      "Not too confident",
      "Somewhat confident",
      "Very confident"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Mean Confidence in Voting Methods by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Confidence in Voting Method",
    color = "Voting Method"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(angle = 35,size = 9),          # smaller y labels
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, size = 9, 
                               hjust = 1),  # tilt x labels
    legend.position = "right"
  )












library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

trust_plot <- svyby(
  ~trust_legislature +
    trust_president +
    trust_congress +
    trust_state_elections +
    trust_local_elections,
  ~IDEO_clean,
  design_issue,
  svymean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)


trust_plot_long <- trust_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(IDEO_clean, starts_with("se.")),
    names_to = "institution",
    values_to = "confidence"
  )

trust_plot_long$institution <- recode(
  trust_plot_long$institution,
  trust_legislature = "State Legislature",
  trust_president = "President",
  trust_congress = "Congress",
  trust_state_elections = "State Elections",
  trust_local_elections = "Local Elections"
)

ggplot(trust_plot_long, aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = confidence, color = institution)) +
  
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1,4),
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Not at all confident",
      "Not too confident",
      "Somewhat confident",
      "Very confident"
    )
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Confidence in Political Institutions by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Confidence",
    color = "Institution"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )
```





```{r}


library(dplyr)
library(survey)



issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    white = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ 1,
      ppethm %in% c(2, 3, 4, 5) ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    black = case_when(
      ppethm == 2 ~ 1,
      ppethm %in% c(1, 3, 4, 5) ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  white = issue$white,
  black = issue$black
)


model_fraud_race_w <- svyglm(
  fraud_perception_rev ~ black + white + dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_fraud_race_w)

model_supp_race_w <- svyglm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ black + dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_supp_race_w)



issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    hispanic = case_when(
      ppethm == 4 ~ 1,
      ppethm %in% c(1, 2, 3, 5) ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
table(issue$hispanic, useNA = "ifany")
table(issue$ppethm, useNA = "ifany")
design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  black = issue$black,
  hispanic = issue$hispanic,
  dem = issue$dem,
  IDEO_clean = issue$IDEO_clean,
  misinformation_perception_rev = issue$misinformation_perception_rev
)

model_misinfo_race_w <- svyglm(
  misinformation_perception_rev ~ black + dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_misinfo_race_w)




library(survey)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

misinfo_plot <- svyby(
  ~misinformation_perception_rev,
  ~IDEO_clean,
  design_issue,
  svymean,
  vartype = "ci",
  na.rm = TRUE
)



ggplot(misinfo_plot,
       aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = misinformation_perception_rev)) +
  
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "darkred") +

  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
  limits = c(1,4),
  breaks = c(1,2,3,4),
  labels = c(
    "Not at all often",
    "Not too often",
    "Somewhat often",
    "Very often"
  )
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Mean Perception of Election Misinformation by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "How Often Misinformation Occurs"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9 ,angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
  )




model_misinfo_ideo <- svyglm(
  misinformation_perception_rev ~ IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_misinfo_ideo)

misinfo_pred <- data.frame(
  IDEO_clean = 1:7
)

pred_out <- predict(
  model_misinfo_ideo,
  newdata = misinfo_pred,
  se.fit = TRUE
)

misinfo_pred$fit <- as.numeric(pred_out)
misinfo_pred$se <- sqrt(attr(pred_out, "var"))
misinfo_pred$lower <- misinfo_pred$fit - 1.96 * misinfo_pred$se
misinfo_pred$upper <- misinfo_pred$fit + 1.96 * misinfo_pred$se




library(ggplot2)

ggplot(misinfo_pred, aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = fit)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
              fill = "darkred", alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 4),
    breaks = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
    labels = c(
      "Not at all often",
      "Not too often",
      "Somewhat often",
      "Very often"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Regression of Misinformation Perception by Ideology",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Predicted Perception of Misinformation Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
  )







library(dplyr)

misinfo_race <- issue %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(misinformation_perception_rev),
    ppethm %in% c(1,2,4)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic"
    )
  )


library(ggplot2)

ggplot(misinfo_race,
       aes(x = misinformation_perception_rev,
           fill = race_group,
           color = race_group)) +

  geom_density(alpha = .25, linewidth = 1) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c(
      "Not at all often",
      "Not too often",
      "Somewhat often",
      "Very often"
    )
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "White" = "gray40",
      "Black" = "darkblue",
      "Hispanic" = "darkorange"
    )
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "White" = "gray40",
      "Black" = "darkblue",
      "Hispanic" = "darkorange"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Perceived Frequency of Election Misinformation by Race",
    x = "Perceived Frequency of Misinformation",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "Race",
    color = "Race"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)








library(dplyr)

misinfo_race <- issue %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(misinformation_perception_rev),
    ppethm %in% c(1,2,4)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic"
    )
  )



library(ggplot2)

ggplot(misinfo_race,
       aes(x = factor(misinformation_perception_rev),
           fill = race_group)) +

  geom_bar(aes(y = after_stat(prop),
               group = race_group),
           position = "dodge") +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format()
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "Not at all often",
      "Not too often",
      "Somewhat often",
      "Very often"
    )
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "White" = "gray40",
      "Black" = "darkblue",
      "Hispanic" = "darkorange"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Perceived Frequency of Election Misinformation by Race",
    x = "How Often Misinformation Occurs",
    y = "Percent of Respondents (Within Race)",
    fill = "Race"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)






```




```{r trus in officials}



design_issue <- update(
  design_issue,
  trust_legislature = issue$trust_legislature,
  trust_president = issue$trust_president,
  trust_congress = issue$trust_congress,
  trust_state_elections = issue$trust_state_elections,
  trust_local_elections = issue$trust_local_elections,
  black = issue$black,
  hispanic = issue$hispanic,
  PARTYID = issue$PARTYID,
  IDEO_clean = issue$IDEO_clean
)


model_trust_legislature <- svyglm(
  trust_legislature ~ black + white + dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_trust_legislature)
#ideology lightly significant liberals are more supportive


model_trust_president <- svyglm(
  trust_president ~ black + white + dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_trust_president)
#trust in president is significant for democrat, ideo, and black




model_trust_congress <- svyglm(
  trust_congress ~ black + hispanic + dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_trust_congress)
#ideo barely significant liberals more trusting of congress


model_trust_state_elections <- svyglm(
  trust_state_elections ~ black + hispanic + dem + IDEO_clean,
  design = design_issue
)

summary(model_trust_state_elections)
#ideology significant more trust from liberals




```





```{R race subsets and ideo}


library(dplyr)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    
    vote_fair_white_black_clean =
      ifelse(vote_fair_white_black %in% c(-1, NA), NA, vote_fair_white_black),
    
    vote_fair_white_hispanic_clean =
      ifelse(vote_fair_white_hispanic %in% c(-1, NA), NA, vote_fair_white_hispanic),
    
    
    fairness_black_binary =
      case_when(
        vote_fair_white_black_clean == 1 ~ 1,
        vote_fair_white_black_clean == 3 ~ 0,
        TRUE ~ NA_real_
      ),
    
    
    fairness_hisp_binary =
      case_when(
        vote_fair_white_hispanic_clean == 1 ~ 1,
        vote_fair_white_hispanic_clean == 3 ~ 0,
        TRUE ~ NA_real_
      )
  )

table(issue$fairness_black_binary, useNA = "ifany")
table(issue$fairness_hisp_binary, useNA = "ifany")



issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    ideology_label = factor(
      IDEO_clean,
      levels = 1:7,
      labels = c(
        "Extremely Liberal",
        "Liberal",
        "Slightly Liberal",
        "Moderate",
        "Slightly Conservative",
        "Conservative",
        "Extremely Conservative"
      )
    )
  )


white_data <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm == 1)

black_data <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm == 2)

hispanic_data <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm == 4)


library(janitor)

white_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(fairness_black_binary), !is.na(ideology_label)) %>%
  tabyl(ideology_label, fairness_black_binary) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)


white_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(fairness_hisp_binary), !is.na(ideology_label)) %>%
tabyl(ideology_label, fairness_hisp_binary) %>%
adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)



black_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(fairness_black_binary), !is.na(ideology_label)) %>%
  tabyl(ideology_label, fairness_black_binary) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)


black_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(fairness_hisp_binary), !is.na(ideology_label)) %>%
  tabyl(ideology_label, fairness_hisp_binary) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)


hispanic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(fairness_black_binary), !is.na(ideology_label)) %>%
  tabyl(ideology_label, fairness_black_binary) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)


hispanic_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(fairness_hisp_binary), !is.na(ideology_label)) %>%
  tabyl(ideology_label, fairness_hisp_binary) %>%
  adorn_percentages("row") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 1)




model_white <- glm(
  fairness_black_binary ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = white_data
)

summary(model_white)
#significant on both counts as would be expected with liberals and dems believing black people have a hard time voting


model_white_hisp <- glm(
  fairness_hisp_binary ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = white_data
)

summary(model_white_hisp)
#significant in expected direction white dems and liberals think its 

model_black_hisp <- glm(
  fairness_hisp_binary ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = black_data
)

summary(model_black_hisp)
#only dem significant democrats are more likely to say white people have it easier than hispanics



model_black <- glm(
  fairness_black_binary ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = black_data
)

summary(model_black)

model_hisp_black <- glm(
  fairness_black_binary ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = hispanic_data
)

summary(model_hisp_black)
#only ideology significant

model_hispanic <- glm(
  fairness_hisp_binary ~ dem + IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = hispanic_data
)

summary(model_hispanic)
#only ideology fully significant








library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

fairness_plot_data <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm %in% c(1, 2, 4)) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    ),
    ideology_label = factor(
      IDEO_clean,
      levels = 1:7,
      labels = c(
        "Extremely Liberal",
        "Liberal",
        "Slightly Liberal",
        "Moderate",
        "Slightly Conservative",
        "Conservative",
        "Extremely Conservative"
      )
    ),
    fairness_black_label = factor(
      fairness_black_binary,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Equal treatment", "Whites advantaged")
    ),
    fairness_hisp_label = factor(
      fairness_hisp_binary,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Equal treatment", "Whites advantaged")
    )
  )



model_prob_black <- glm(
  fairness_black_binary ~ IDEO_clean * race_group,
  family = binomial,
  data = fairness_plot_data %>% filter(!is.na(fairness_black_binary), !is.na(IDEO_clean))
)

pred_black <- expand.grid(
  IDEO_clean = 1:7,
  race_group = c("White respondents", "Black respondents", "Hispanic respondents")
)

pred_black_out <- predict(
  model_prob_black,
  newdata = pred_black,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

pred_black$fit_link <- pred_black_out$fit
pred_black$se_link <- pred_black_out$se.fit
pred_black$fit <- plogis(pred_black$fit_link)
pred_black$lower <- plogis(pred_black$fit_link - 1.96 * pred_black$se_link)
pred_black$upper <- plogis(pred_black$fit_link + 1.96 * pred_black$se_link)

ggplot(pred_black, aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = fit, color = race_group, fill = race_group)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Saying Whites Have an Advantage in Voting\n(White vs Black Fairness Question)",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Respondent group",
    fill = "Respondent group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
  )








model_prob_hisp <- glm(
  fairness_hisp_binary ~ IDEO_clean * race_group,
  family = binomial,
  data = fairness_plot_data %>% filter(!is.na(fairness_hisp_binary), !is.na(IDEO_clean))
)

pred_hisp <- expand.grid(
  IDEO_clean = 1:7,
  race_group = c("White respondents", "Black respondents", "Hispanic respondents")
)

pred_hisp_out <- predict(
  model_prob_hisp,
  newdata = pred_hisp,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

pred_hisp$fit_link <- pred_hisp_out$fit
pred_hisp$se_link <- pred_hisp_out$se.fit
pred_hisp$fit <- plogis(pred_hisp$fit_link)
pred_hisp$lower <- plogis(pred_hisp$fit_link - 1.96 * pred_hisp$se_link)
pred_hisp$upper <- plogis(pred_hisp$fit_link + 1.96 * pred_hisp$se_link)

ggplot(pred_hisp, aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = fit, color = race_group, fill = race_group)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Saying Whites Have an Advantage in Voting\n(White vs Hispanic Fairness Question)",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Respondent group",
    fill = "Respondent group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
  )







black_check <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm == 2,
         !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
         !is.na(IDEO_clean)) %>%
  group_by(IDEO_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    pct_whites_advantaged = mean(fairness_black_binary, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

black_check



black_check_w <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm == 2,
         !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
         !is.na(IDEO_clean),
         !is.na(WEIGHT)) %>%
  group_by(IDEO_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    weighted_pct = weighted.mean(fairness_black_binary, WEIGHT, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )

black_check_w



ggplot(black_check_w, aes(x = IDEO_clean, y = weighted_pct)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Black Respondents: Raw Weighted Probability of Saying Whites Are Advantaged",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Weighted Percent"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))




black_only <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm == 2,
         !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
         !is.na(IDEO_clean))

model_black_only <- glm(
  fairness_black_binary ~ IDEO_clean,
  family = binomial,
  data = black_only
)

summary(model_black_only)
#not significant with just ideology




library(dplyr)

fairness_plot_data <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm %in% c(1,2,4)) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    ),
    
    ideology_label = factor(
      IDEO_clean,
      levels = 1:7,
      labels = c(
        "Extremely Liberal",
        "Liberal",
        "Slightly Liberal",
        "Moderate",
        "Slightly Conservative",
        "Conservative",
        "Extremely Conservative"
      )
    )
  )



design_fair <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~WEIGHT,
  data = fairness_plot_data
)
model_prob_black_w <- svyglm(
  fairness_black_binary ~ IDEO_clean * race_group,
  design = design_fair,
  family = quasibinomial()
)


pred_black <- expand.grid(
  IDEO_clean = 1:7,
  race_group = c(
    "White respondents",
    "Black respondents",
    "Hispanic respondents"
  )
)

pred_out <- predict(
  model_prob_black_w,
  newdata = pred_black,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

pred_black$fit_link <- as.numeric(pred_out)
pred_black$se_link <- sqrt(attr(pred_out, "var"))

pred_black$fit <- plogis(pred_black$fit_link)
pred_black$lower <- plogis(pred_black$fit_link - 1.96 * pred_black$se_link)
pred_black$upper <- plogis(pred_black$fit_link + 1.96 * pred_black$se_link)




library(ggplot2)

ggplot(pred_black,
       aes(x = IDEO_clean,
           y = fit,
           color = race_group,
           fill = race_group)) +

  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
              alpha = .15,
              color = NA) +

  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Weighted Predicted Probability Easier for White people to vote
    Compared to Black or Equal",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Respondent group",
    fill = "Respondent group"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )









library(dplyr)
library(survey)

fairness_plot_data <- issue %>%
  filter(ppethm %in% c(1, 2, 4)) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(fairness_hisp_binary),
    !is.na(IDEO_clean),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  )

design_fair_hisp <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~WEIGHT,
  data = fairness_plot_data
)

model_prob_hisp_w <- svyglm(
  fairness_hisp_binary ~ IDEO_clean * race_group,
  design = design_fair_hisp,
  family = quasibinomial()
)

pred_hisp <- expand.grid(
  IDEO_clean = 1:7,
  race_group = c(
    "White respondents",
    "Black respondents",
    "Hispanic respondents"
  )
)

pred_out <- predict(
  model_prob_hisp_w,
  newdata = pred_hisp,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

pred_hisp$fit_link <- as.numeric(pred_out)
pred_hisp$se_link <- sqrt(attr(pred_out, "var"))

pred_hisp$fit <- plogis(pred_hisp$fit_link)
pred_hisp$lower <- plogis(pred_hisp$fit_link - 1.96 * pred_hisp$se_link)
pred_hisp$upper <- plogis(pred_hisp$fit_link + 1.96 * pred_hisp$se_link)


library(ggplot2)

ggplot(pred_hisp,
       aes(x = IDEO_clean,
           y = fit,
           color = race_group,
           fill = race_group)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
              alpha = 0.15,
              color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weighted Predicted Probability of Saying Whites Have an Advantage in Voting\n(White vs Hispanic Fairness Question)",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Respondent group",
    fill = "Respondent group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )









library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

fairness_black_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(
    ppethm %in% c(1, 2, 4),
    !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
    !is.na(IDEO_clean),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    ),
    fairness_black_label = factor(
      fairness_black_binary,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Equal treatment", "Whites advantaged")
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean, fairness_black_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wt = sum(WEIGHT),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean) %>%
  mutate(
    percent = wt / sum(wt)
  )

ggplot(fairness_black_plot,
       aes(x = factor(IDEO_clean),
           y = percent,
           fill = fairness_black_label)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~race_group) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Equal treatment" = "gray70",
      "Whites advantaged" = "steelblue"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weighted Beliefs About Voting Fairness by Ideology\n(White vs Black Fairness Question)",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Response"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
  )



fairness_hisp_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(
    ppethm %in% c(1, 2, 4),
    !is.na(fairness_hisp_binary),
    !is.na(IDEO_clean),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    ),
    fairness_hisp_label = factor(
      fairness_hisp_binary,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Equal treatment", "Whites advantaged")
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean, fairness_hisp_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wt = sum(WEIGHT),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean) %>%
  mutate(
    percent = wt / sum(wt)
  )

ggplot(fairness_hisp_plot,
       aes(x = factor(IDEO_clean),
           y = percent,
           fill = fairness_hisp_label)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~race_group) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Equal treatment" = "gray70",
      "Whites advantaged" = "darkorange"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weighted Beliefs About Voting Fairness by Ideology\n(White vs Hispanic Fairness Question)",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Response"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
  )





library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

fairness_black_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(
    ppethm %in% c(1,2,4),
    !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
    !is.na(IDEO_clean),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    ),
    fairness_black_label = factor(
      fairness_black_binary,
      levels = c(0,1),
      labels = c("Equal treatment","Whites advantaged")
    )
  )

# weighted percentages
fairness_black_stack <- fairness_black_plot %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean, fairness_black_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wt = sum(WEIGHT),
    .groups="drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean) %>%
  mutate(percent = wt/sum(wt))

# unweighted counts
fairness_black_n <- fairness_black_plot %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups="drop")

ggplot(fairness_black_stack,
       aes(x=factor(IDEO_clean),
           y=percent,
           fill=fairness_black_label)) +

  geom_col() +

  geom_text(
    data = fairness_black_n,
    aes(x=factor(IDEO_clean),
        y=1.05,
        label=paste0("N=",n)),
    inherit.aes=FALSE,
    size=3
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~race_group) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels=scales::percent_format(),
    limits=c(0,1.1)
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(
    labels=c(
      "Extremely Liberal",
      "Liberal",
      "Slightly Liberal",
      "Moderate",
      "Slightly Conservative",
      "Conservative",
      "Extremely Conservative"
    )
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values=c(
      "Equal treatment"="gray70",
      "Whites advantaged"="steelblue"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title="Weighted Beliefs About Voting Fairness by Ideology\n(White vs Black Question)",
    x="Ideology",
    y="Percent",
    fill="Response"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=35,hjust=1))





library(dplyr)

n_black_fairness <- issue %>%
  filter(
    ppethm %in% c(1,2,4),
    !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
    !is.na(IDEO_clean)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(race_group, IDEO_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

n_black_fairness


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

count_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(
    ppethm %in% c(1,2,4),
    !is.na(IDEO_clean)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    ),
    ideology_label = factor(
      IDEO_clean,
      levels = 1:7,
      labels = c(
        "Extremely Liberal",
        "Liberal",
        "Slightly Liberal",
        "Moderate",
        "Slightly Conservative",
        "Conservative",
        "Extremely Conservative"
      )
    )
  )

ggplot(count_plot,
       aes(x = ideology_label)) +

  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +

  facet_wrap(~race_group) +

  labs(
    title = "Number of Respondents by Ideology and Race",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Count"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )






library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

count_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(
    ppethm %in% c(1,2,4),
    !is.na(IDEO_clean)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_group = case_when(
      ppethm == 1 ~ "White respondents",
      ppethm == 2 ~ "Black respondents",
      ppethm == 4 ~ "Hispanic respondents"
    ),
    ideology_label = factor(
      IDEO_clean,
      levels = 1:7,
      labels = c(
        "Extremely Liberal",
        "Liberal",
        "Slightly Liberal",
        "Moderate",
        "Slightly Conservative",
        "Conservative",
        "Extremely Conservative"
      )
    )
  )

ggplot(count_plot,
       aes(x = ideology_label)) +

  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +

  geom_text(
    stat = "count",
    aes(label = after_stat(count)),
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 4
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~race_group) +

  labs(
    title = "Number of Respondents by Ideology and Race",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Count"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )



```






```{r conservative suppression}


library(dplyr)

cons_subset <- issue %>%
  filter(
    IDEO_clean %in% c(6,7),
    !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
    !is.na(suppression_perception_rev)
  )





table(cons_subset$IDEO_clean)



model_cons_suppression_w <- lm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ fairness_black_binary,
  data = cons_subset,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model_cons_suppression_w)

#people who think white people are advantaged are more likely to have easier time voting think there is more suppresstion?



extreme_cons_subset <- issue %>%
  filter(
    IDEO_clean == 7,
    !is.na(fairness_black_binary),
    !is.na(suppression_perception_rev),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  )

nrow(extreme_cons_subset)


model_extreme_cons_suppression_w <- lm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ fairness_black_binary,
  data = extreme_cons_subset,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model_extreme_cons_suppression_w)




library(dplyr)

cons_plot <- issue %>%
  filter(
    IDEO_clean %in% c(5,6,7),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ideology_label = factor(
      IDEO_clean,
      levels = c(5,6,7),
      labels = c(
        "Slightly Conservative",
        "Conservative",
        "Extremely Conservative"
      )
    )
  )


cons_black <- cons_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(vote_fair_white_black)) %>%
  mutate(
    fairness_black_label = factor(
      vote_fair_white_black,
      levels = c(1,2,3),
      labels = c(
        "Easier for Whites",
        "Easier for Blacks",
        "Equal treatment"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(ideology_label, fairness_black_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wt = sum(WEIGHT),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(ideology_label) %>%
  mutate(percent = wt/sum(wt))

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(cons_black,
       aes(x = ideology_label,
           y = percent,
           fill = fairness_black_label)) +

  geom_col() +

  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Easier for Whites" = "steelblue",
      "Easier for Blacks" = "firebrick",
      "Equal treatment" = "gray70"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Conservative Respondents: Who Has an Easier Time Voting?\n(White vs Black)",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Response"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)



cons_hisp <- cons_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(vote_fair_white_hispanic)) %>%
  mutate(
    fairness_hisp_label = factor(
      vote_fair_white_hispanic,
      levels = c(1,2,3),
      labels = c(
        "Easier for Whites",
        "Easier for Hispanics",
        "Equal treatment"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(ideology_label, fairness_hisp_label) %>%
  summarise(
    wt = sum(WEIGHT),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(ideology_label) %>%
  mutate(percent = wt/sum(wt))

ggplot(cons_hisp,
       aes(x = ideology_label,
           y = percent,
           fill = fairness_hisp_label)) +

  geom_col() +

  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Easier for Whites" = "steelblue",
      "Easier for Hispanics" = "darkorange",
      "Equal treatment" = "gray70"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Conservative Respondents: Who Has an Easier Time Voting?\n(White vs Hispanic)",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Response"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)













library(dplyr)
library(MASS)

issue <- issue %>%
  mutate(
    fairness_black_factor = case_when(
      vote_fair_white_black == 1 ~ "Whites advantaged",
      vote_fair_white_black == 2 ~ "Blacks advantaged",
      vote_fair_white_black == 3 ~ "Equal treatment",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    fairness_hisp_factor = case_when(
      vote_fair_white_hispanic == 1 ~ "Whites advantaged",
      vote_fair_white_hispanic == 2 ~ "Hispanics advantaged",
      vote_fair_white_hispanic == 3 ~ "Equal treatment",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

issue$fairness_black_factor <- factor(
  issue$fairness_black_factor,
  levels = c("Equal treatment", "Whites advantaged", "Blacks advantaged")
)

issue$fairness_hisp_factor <- factor(
  issue$fairness_hisp_factor,
  levels = c("Equal treatment", "Whites advantaged", "Hispanics advantaged")
)


cons_general <- issue %>%
  filter(
    IDEO_clean %in% c(5, 6, 7),
    !is.na(suppression_perception_rev),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  )

extreme_cons <- issue %>%
  filter(
    IDEO_clean == 7,
    !is.na(suppression_perception_rev),
    !is.na(WEIGHT)
  )


model_cons_black <- lm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ fairness_black_factor,
  data = cons_general,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model_cons_black)
#in conservative those who think whites are advantaged over blacks are more likely to think there is more suppression


model_extreme_black <- lm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ fairness_black_factor,
  data = extreme_cons,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model_extreme_black)
#even in extereme conservative people who think whites are advantaged are more likely to think there is more suppression



model_cons_hisp <- lm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ fairness_hisp_factor,
  data = cons_general,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model_cons_hisp)
#in general conservatism people who think white people are advantaged think there is more suppression



model_extreme_hisp <- lm(
  suppression_perception_rev ~ fairness_hisp_factor,
  data = extreme_cons,
  weights = WEIGHT
)

summary(model_extreme_hisp)
#nothing significant



library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

pred_all <- pred_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    institution = stringr::str_wrap(institution, width = 18)
  )

ggplot(pred_all,
       aes(x = x, y = fit, color = type, group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.08) +
  facet_wrap(~institution) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:4,
    labels = c("Rarely", "Occasionally", "Somewhat often", "Very often")
  ) +
```
